project.name: pokemon-tactics
project.description: Tactical RPG game (Pokemon x Final Fantasy Tactics) — TypeScript monorepo with pure game engine core, data package, and Phaser 4 renderer

nodes.name: ai-player.md
nodes.summary: Agent definition for the AI player sub-agent responsible for simulating automated Pokemon tactical decisions during headless combat testing.

nodes.name: asset-manager.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive agent specification for managing game assets including Pokemon sprite pipelines from PMDCollab, PixelLab MCP tile generation, naming conventions, and isometric style guidelines.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers both procedural sprite downloading and AI-generative tile creation workflows via PixelLab MCP.

nodes.name: balancer.md
nodes.summary: Agent definition for the game balancer sub-agent that runs headless combat simulations to measure and report on Pokemon/move balance metrics.

nodes.name: best-practices.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the best-practices researcher sub-agent that performs web searches and conformity audits against established patterns for rendering, physics, and game architecture.

nodes.name: ci-setup.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for configuring GitHub Actions CI pipelines including build, lint, typecheck, and test stages with caching optimizations for the pnpm monorepo.

nodes.name: code-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Detailed agent specification for the code reviewer sub-agent covering blocking checks for naming, enums, file structure, TypeScript strictness, test coverage, dead code, and architecture conformance.

nodes.name: commit-message.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for generating conventional commit messages after running the CI gate, reading plan context, and verifying the diff — title-only format enforced.

nodes.name: core-guardian.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the core-guardian sub-agent that enforces architectural boundaries of the pure game engine package — no UI imports, no side effects, no circular dependencies.

nodes.name: data-miner-knowledge.md
nodes.summary: Persistent knowledge base for the data-miner agent capturing stable API URLs (Showdown, PokeAPI, PMDCollab), naming conventions, parsing gotchas, and stat formulas used when sourcing Pokemon data.

nodes.name: data-miner.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the data-miner sub-agent that extracts official Pokemon stats, moves, and abilities from Showdown/PokeAPI and formats them for the project data package.

nodes.name: debugger-knowledge.md
nodes.summary: Persistent knowledge base for the debugger agent containing action-flow entry points, file categories by bug type, diagnostic commands, known gotchas, and reproduction seeds for the tactical game engine.

nodes.name: debugger.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the debugger sub-agent covering systematic investigation methodology, common bug categories in tactical games, and chrome-devtools MCP usage for visual/runtime issues.

nodes.name: dependency-manager.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for managing pnpm monorepo dependencies including pre-addition checks, regular audits, and monorepo-specific rules for keeping core package dependency-free.

nodes.name: doc-keeper.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the doc-keeper sub-agent responsible for keeping STATUS.md, implementations tracker, roadmap, and README counters in sync after every plan execution.

nodes.name: feedback-triager.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the feedback-triager sub-agent that reads GitHub issues, classifies them by type/priority, detects duplicates, verifies relevance to current roadmap phase, and proposes action plans.

nodes.name: game-designer.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the game-designer reviewer sub-agent that analyzes data coherence, balance metrics, and tactical fun during plan review — focusing on Pokemon game design principles.

nodes.name: level-designer.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive agent specification for creating isometric Tiled maps covering tileset constraints, layer ordering, height formulas, slope/ramp rules, multi-format spawn objectgroups, and tactical balance guidelines.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes detailed isometric coordinate math, ramp placement rules, fall damage thresholds, and ASCII preview workflow before writing TMX.

nodes.name: move-pattern-designer-knowledge.md
nodes.summary: Persistent knowledge base for the move-pattern-designer agent recording settled design decisions, available attack patterns in the codebase, decision heuristics, and creative director preferences.

nodes.name: move-pattern-designer.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for assigning tactical attack patterns to Pokemon moves, covering available pattern types, semantic keyword mapping, 2v2 heuristics, special effects, and escalation rules.

nodes.name: performance-profiler.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the performance-profiler sub-agent analyzing runtime FPS/memory via chrome-devtools MCP, bundle size via vite-bundle-visualizer, and headless core benchmarks with recommended thresholds.

nodes.name: plan-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the plan-reviewer sub-agent that validates and creates implementation plans against the project's plan format template, reviews for completeness, and maintains plan status.

nodes.name: publisher.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the publisher sub-agent that verifies GitHub release drafts, validates the production build, and publishes releases after human approval with wiki and reference updates.

nodes.name: release-drafter.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the release-drafter sub-agent that maintains GitHub release drafts by appending changelog entries following semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) with categorized sections.

nodes.name: sandbox-json.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the sandbox-json sub-agent that generates JSON test scenario configurations for the renderer sandbox, specifying player and dummy Pokemon with validated move and ability IDs.

nodes.name: session-closer.md
nodes.summary: Agent specification for the session-closer sub-agent that updates STATUS.md, reconciles the backlog against recent commits, and triggers the CI gate and commit-message agent chain at session end.

nodes.name: test-writer.md
nodes.summary: Agent definition for the test-writer sub-agent, specifying how to write unit and integration tests for move mechanics, ability hooks, and cross-cutting battle mechanics using Vitest and the project's testing factories.

nodes.name: visual-analyst.md
nodes.summary: Agent definition for the visual-analyst sub-agent, describing how to analyze screenshots for visual style, UI/UX, grid layout, and animations with reference to FFTA and related game aesthetics.

nodes.name: visual-tester-knowledge.md
nodes.summary: Persistent knowledge base for the visual-tester agent, capturing UI layout, navigation flows, button coordinates, grid positions, known gotchas, and performance strategies for efficient Playwright-based visual testing.

nodes.name: visual-tester.md
nodes.summary: Agent definition for the visual-tester sub-agent, providing a detailed step-by-step workflow for launching the dev server, navigating the game, taking screenshots, checking console errors, and producing structured visual test reports.

nodes.name: wiki-keeper.md
nodes.summary: Agent definition for the wiki-keeper sub-agent, responsible for detecting relevant code changes and updating the project's public GitHub wiki submodule with consistent, player-facing documentation.

nodes.name: block-forbidden-commands.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code PreToolUse hook that intercepts Bash tool calls and blocks forbidden operations including git versioning commands (commit/push/add), global package installs, nvm/npm config changes, dangerous system operations (recursive rm, sudo, chmod 777), and pipe-to-shell supply-chain attacks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses character-class regex obfuscation (e.g. [p]npm, [u]sh) to prevent string-matching security scanners from triggering false positives on the hook source itself.

nodes.name: core.md
nodes.summary: Coding rules for the core package, enforcing zero UI dependencies, mandatory unit tests for all public exports, use of project testing mocks, const-object enum pattern, one-interface-per-file, and handler-based move effect registration.

nodes.name: data.md
nodes.summary: Coding rules for the data package, covering kebab-case IDs, mandatory bilingual names, official base stats, Gen-1-only roster constraint, local reference knowledge base usage, and Pokemon Champions stat alignment.

nodes.name: quality.md
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting quality rules governing Biome linter usage, naming conventions (kebab-case for Tiled identifiers, PascalCase for types, CONSTANT_CASE for constants), and a zero-tolerance policy for lint warnings.

nodes.name: renderer-babylon.md
nodes.summary: Coding rules specific to the Babylon.js renderer spike, covering deep import requirements for tree-shaking, GridMaterial UV offset, pixel-art texture settings, UV atlas coordinate calculation, rendering group depth management, and design system constants.

nodes.name: renderer.md
nodes.summary: Coding rules for the Phaser renderer package, requiring colors and depth constants in constants.ts, documented design system entries, and import of core via package alias rather than relative paths.

nodes.name: tests.md
nodes.summary: Coding rules for test files, mandating no comments in unit tests, Gherkin blocks for scenario tests, use of project factories, buildMoveTestEngine helper, co-location with source, and .test.ts naming convention.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code project settings defining PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks (including the block-forbidden-commands hook), an extensive allowlist of permitted Bash and MCP tool patterns, and tool-level permission rules.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Definition of the /next skill that reads docs/next.md, STATUS.md, the roadmap, and the current plan to present the next recommended development step and any deferred items.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Definition of the /review-local skill that runs a code review against local git diff changes, applying project coding standards and architecture rules.

nodes.name: bug_report.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template for bug reports, collecting reproduction steps, expected and actual behaviour, and environment information with a structured form body.

nodes.name: config.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template configuration disabling blank issues to enforce use of structured templates.

nodes.name: feature_request.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template for feature requests, capturing the motivation, desired behaviour, and any alternative solutions considered.

nodes.name: general_feedback.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template for general feedback and open-ended comments not fitting the bug or feature categories.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow triggered on push and pull requests to main, running pnpm lint, typecheck, unit tests, and integration tests on Node 24.

nodes.name: deploy.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions deployment workflow triggered on release publication, building the project with pnpm and deploying the renderer dist output to GitHub Pages.

nodes.name: .npmrc
nodes.summary: npm/pnpm runtime configuration file, typically setting registry or workspace-related options for the monorepo.

nodes.name: biome.json
nodes.summary: Biome linter and formatter configuration for the entire monorepo, enabling strict linting rules across all packages with custom overrides for test files and generated data, replacing ESLint and Prettier.
nodes.languageNotes: Biome v2 format with 9 top-level configuration sections and per-path overrides; warnings are treated as errors in CI via `biome ci packages/`.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Primary Claude Code instructions file for the project, defining roles, documentation reading order, development principles, conventions, tech stack, forbidden actions, and the full agent orchestration strategy including automated chains and skill commands.

nodes.name: CREDITS.md
nodes.summary: Asset attribution file listing credits for Pokemon sprites and portraits used in the project, with per-Pokemon artist credits and applicable license terms.

nodes.name: abilities-system.md
nodes.summary: Technical reference for the abilities (Talents) system, covering the AbilityHandler architecture, AbilityActivated event emission pattern, anti-spam flags, aura hooks, position-linked statuses, call sites, and a step-by-step guide for adding new abilities.

nodes.name: agent-orchestration.md
nodes.summary: Defines the Claude Code multi-agent orchestration model for the project, cataloguing all agents by trigger mode (auto, propose-first, explicit-only), foreground/background rules, main workflow chains, and the persistent agenda in docs/next.md.

nodes.name: ai-system.md
nodes.summary: Documents the AI battle system architecture, including the scoring pipeline (scoreAction, pickScoredAction), difficulty levels, negative score guards, move-reach calculation, and current limitations of the AiTeamController.

nodes.name: architecture.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive technical architecture reference covering the decoupled core/renderer principle, monorepo structure, attack composition system, event pipeline, sandbox mode, i18n system, data override pipeline, sprite pipeline, replay system, and Claude Code agent tooling.

nodes.name: backlog.md
nodes.summary: Living backlog tracking active bugs, IA improvement notes, data issues, visual feedback gaps, future feature ideas, and a resolved section — cross-referenced with plan numbers and commit hashes for traceability.

nodes.name: decisions.md
nodes.summary: Immutable log of all architectural and game-design decisions taken (numbered sequentially), plus open questions and discarded alternatives, providing a permanent rationale record for the project.

nodes.name: design-system.md
nodes.summary: Authoritative visual design reference covering artistic direction, color palettes (semantic and by UI component), depth constants, typography, grid constants, cursor behavior, decoration layering, and the turn-timeline CT widget — mandatory reading before touching renderer visuals.

nodes.name: game-design.md
nodes.summary: Master game-design document defining the full tactical combat ruleset: CT/round-robin turn order, damage formula, grid terrain types with traversal and accuracy rules, AoE targeting, status effects, knockback, multi-hit, abilities, items, placement phase, and long-term game modes.

nodes.name: glossary.md
nodes.summary: Project glossary defining game-design terms (CT, AoE, terrain types, status categories), technical vocabulary, and architecture-specific terms used across all project documentation.

nodes.name: headless-test-results.md
nodes.summary: Records two headless AI combat test runs (Random AI vs Smart heuristic AI) with observations on game-loop correctness, KO handling, and win condition detection — used to validate the core engine before the renderer was built.

nodes.name: implementations.md
nodes.summary: Living tracker of implemented content: 151 Gen-1 Pokemon (with Mega-Evolutions), 127 moves, 44 abilities, and 12 held items — each marked implemented (✓) or pending (✗) with counters synced to README.md.

nodes.name: isometric-height-rendering.md
nodes.summary: Deep technical reference for the isometric height rendering system: projection formulas, Tiled multi-layer conventions, heightmap calculation, sprite Y positioning, depth sorting, movement animation with asymmetric easing, screen-to-grid picking algorithm, and key design decisions.

nodes.name: methodology.md
nodes.summary: Documents the project's human-Claude working methodology: role definitions, documentation organisation, feature and bug workflows, code conventions, test levels, Git conventions, visual validation approach, and decision-making process.

nodes.name: multiplayer.md
nodes.summary: Architecture design for a future P2P WebRTC multiplayer mode, covering the lockstep principle, connection flow, action protocol messages, anti-cheat via hash verification, disconnection handling, and a phased matchmaking roadmap.

nodes.name: next.md
nodes.summary: Persistent session agenda maintained by Claude Code, listing the current immediate tasks, deferred items, recently completed work, and next-session context — read via the /next skill.

nodes.name: 001-setup-monorepo.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan for initialising the pnpm monorepo with packages/core, packages/data, and packages/renderer, configuring TypeScript project references, Vitest, Biome, and the Vite dev server.

nodes.name: 002-core-models.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan defining the foundational core data models: Pokemon, Move, Tile, GridMap, BattleState, and the const-object enum pattern for all game enumerations.

nodes.name: 003-battle-engine-data.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan building the minimal BattleEngine (TurnManager, getLegalActions, submitAction for move and skip), the packages/data package with base Pokemon/move data, startup validation, and a headless test script.

nodes.name: 004-effect-resolution.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan implementing damage calculation (stat stage multipliers, STAB, type chart, critical hits), accuracy checks, status-effect processors, and a full combat integration test.

nodes.name: 005-turn-phases-effect-registry.md
nodes.summary: Completed refactor plan introducing the effect-handler registry pattern (replacing switch/case), formal turn-phase enum (StartTurn/Action/EndTurn), dynamic initiative recalculation, and BattleEngine boucle while refactor.

nodes.name: 006-battle-loop.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan wiring the full battle loop: status-tick handlers (StartTurn), Leech Seed drain (EndTurn), KO handling, victory condition, paralysis filtering, and comprehensive integration tests.

nodes.name: 007-renderer-poc.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan building the first visual renderer POC with Phaser: isometric grid, placeholder Pokemon sprites, unit selection with tile highlighting, move/attack execution, hot-seat two-player mode, and minimal UI.

nodes.name: 008-move-plus-act.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan enabling FFTA-style move-then-attack in one turn: replacing SkipTurn with EndTurn, per-turn action-state tracking, conditional getLegalActions, and renderer adaptation for the two-phase input flow.

nodes.name: 009-ui-fft-like.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan adding FFT-style UI chrome: animated tile cursor, info panel, contextual action menu, attack sub-menu, InputState refactor, turn-order timeline, sprite polish, and victory screen fix.

nodes.name: 010-pmdcollab-sprites.md
nodes.summary: Completed plan for integrating PMDCollab animated sprites into the Phaser renderer: extraction script producing texture atlases, credit/licence handling, Phaser preload, PokemonSprite refactor from circle to animated sprite, and portrait integration in the UI.

nodes.name: 011-ko-body-blocking.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for permanent KO mechanics: KO'd Pokemon bodies remain on the grid as obstacles, BFS pathfinding traverses them, and the koCountdown field is removed from PokemonInstance.

nodes.name: 012-end-turn-direction.md
nodes.summary: Plan for adding mandatory end-of-turn direction selection, requiring players to choose a facing direction after each action, with FFT-style UI and state machine integration.

nodes.name: 013-map-model-placement.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive plan establishing the MapDefinition data model and PlacementPhase, covering core data types, map validation, spawn zones, and the full renderer placement UI flow.

nodes.name: 014-slash-blast-patterns.md
nodes.summary: Plan for implementing Slash and Blast attack patterns, adding their resolvers to BattleEngine.getValidTargetPositions and updating tactical.ts with 8 new pattern assignments.

nodes.name: 015-stats-level-50.md
nodes.summary: Plan for computing and storing level-50 battle stats using the official Pokemon formula, ensuring all roster Pokemon have correct HP, Attack, Defense, and Speed values.

nodes.name: 016-attack-info-ui-type-icons-cone-fix.md
nodes.summary: Plan for adding attack info UI elements including Pokemon type icons, a 5x5 mini-grid pattern preview per attack, and fixing the cone targeting pattern bug.

nodes.name: 017-aoe-preview.md
nodes.summary: Detailed plan for AoE attack previsualization on the grid, covering hover-based preview, lock-and-confirm flow, sprite blinking for targets, and pattern-specific behavior for all attack shapes.

nodes.name: 018-status-icons-hp-bar-sleep-anim.md
nodes.summary: Plan for visual polish including ZA-style status icons in the turn timeline and on sprites, FFTIC-style HP bars, Showdown-style stat-change badges, and PMD sleep animations.

nodes.name: 019-damage-estimate-preview.md
nodes.summary: Plan for displaying estimated damage preview with random-roll range before an attack is confirmed, exposing a calculation utility from the battle core.

nodes.name: 020-responsive-scaling-camera.md
nodes.summary: Plan for responsive canvas scaling using Phaser FIT mode, plus camera zoom (3 levels) and pan support, targeting 4K displays with pixel-art fidelity.

nodes.name: 021-sprite-offsets-shadows.md
nodes.summary: Plan for fixing sprite positioning using Shadow.png and Offsets.png data from PMDCollab, and adding drop shadows beneath Pokemon sprites on the isometric grid.

nodes.name: 022-timeline-turn-order.md
nodes.summary: Plan for redesigning the turn-order timeline UI component, improving portrait sizing and visual clarity of the initiative queue.

nodes.name: 023-sandbox-mode.md
nodes.summary: Plan for a sandbox/training mode featuring a mini-map, a passive training-dummy AI, 8 defensive move implementations in core, and configuration panels for rapid gameplay testing.

nodes.name: 024-bugfixes-menu-relocation.md
nodes.summary: Plan addressing two sandbox bugs (Leech Seed HP bar and burn-on-spawn) and relocating the attack action menu to the bottom-left corner of the screen.

nodes.name: 025-move-integration-tests.md
nodes.summary: Plan for establishing per-move integration test files using the buildMoveTestEngine factory, covering all currently implemented moves with Gherkin-style Given/When/Then scenarios.

nodes.name: 026-roster-expansion-phase1.md
nodes.summary: Large plan introducing 6 new core battle mechanics (badly_poisoned, confusion, bind, knockback, multi-hit, recharge) with detailed specifications, move tables, and step-by-step implementation tasks.

nodes.name: 027-new-pokemon-roster.md
nodes.summary: Plan for adding 8 new Gen-1 Pokemon (Eevee, Tentacool, Nidoran-M, Meowth, Magnemite, Sandshrew, Lickitung, Kangaskhan) with movesets, sprites, and data entries.

nodes.name: 028-deterministic-replay.md
nodes.summary: Plan for deterministic battle replay using a seeded PRNG, enabling reproducible combat sequences for testing, debugging, and future AI training.

nodes.name: 029-ai-difficulty-levels.md
nodes.summary: Plan for AI difficulty tiers (easy/medium/hard), expanding the AI scoring and action-selection system to support configurable aggression and decision quality.

nodes.name: 030-i18n-fr-en.md
nodes.summary: Plan for adding French/English internationalisation support, covering move names, Pokemon names, and UI strings with a locale-switching mechanism.

nodes.name: 031-battle-visual-feedback.md
nodes.summary: Plan for battle visual feedback polish including floating damage/status text (BattleText), knockback slide animation, confusion wobble, and refactoring Leech Seed and Bind into proper volatile status effects.

nodes.name: 032-variable-movement-by-speed.md
nodes.summary: Plan for making movement range per-Pokemon based on the Speed stat, replacing the fixed movement value with a formula derived from the Pokemon's level-50 speed.

nodes.name: 033-team-select.md
nodes.summary: Plan for a team selection screen allowing players to pick their roster before a battle, with a dex-based grid UI and confirmation flow leading into the placement phase.

nodes.name: 034-remove-sandbox.md
nodes.summary: Short plan to remove URL query-param sandbox access and block direct sandbox boot from the browser, in preparation for a dedicated CLI-based sandbox workflow.

nodes.name: 035-sandbox-cli-json.md
nodes.summary: Plan for replacing URL-param sandbox access with a CLI-driven JSON config approach: environment variable boot flag, JSON file/inline argument parsing, and a SandboxPanel export button.

nodes.name: 036-main-menu-settings.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for the main menu, battle mode sub-menu, settings scene, and disclaimer/credits scene, covering infrastructure for persistent settings storage and 6 phased renderer steps.

nodes.name: 037-battle-log.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for a scrollable battle log panel in the renderer that displays combat events, move names, and damage numbers in real time.

nodes.name: 038-enemy-move-range-hover.md
nodes.summary: Plan to display enemy Pokemon movement range and attack range tiles on hover, providing tactical awareness of threat zones during the player's turn.

nodes.name: 039-battle-animations-direction-category.md
nodes.summary: Plan to add directional sprite support (4 or 8 directions) and move-category-based animations (physical vs. special vs. status) to the battle renderer, including a sprite pipeline update.

nodes.name: 040-multi-team-hot-seat.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive plan to extend the game from a fixed 2-team model to support 2–12 teams in hot-seat mode, requiring changes across core PlayerId, map spawn formats, renderer team colors, and TeamSelectScene layout.

nodes.name: 041-goatcounter-analytics.md
nodes.summary: Minimal plan to integrate privacy-friendly Goatcounter analytics via a Vite plugin, tracking page views without collecting personal data.

nodes.name: 042-bugfixes-feedback-session.md
nodes.summary: Post-playtest bug-fix plan addressing 5 issues: team-colored HP bars, damage preview overlay, status badge borders, spacebar end-turn shortcut, and AI vs. AI missing victory screen.

nodes.name: 043-arena-tileset-renderer.md
nodes.summary: Plan to replace procedurally drawn grid tiles with a Pokemon arena sprite tileset, migrating IsometricGrid from Phaser Graphics to Sprite objects and composing the arena map.

nodes.name: 044-pixel-art-mode-font.md
nodes.summary: Plan to enable Phaser's global pixelArt mode, remove redundant manual NEAREST scale settings, preserve LINEAR filtering for portrait images, and integrate the Pokemon Emerald Pro bitmap font.

nodes.name: 045-tiled-map-format-parser-validation-preview.md
nodes.summary: Plan to define the canonical Tiled map format, implement a `parseTiledMap` parser with 11 terrain types, business validation, spawn layer parsing, and a renderer preview mode.

nodes.name: 046-height-elevation-fall-damage.md
nodes.summary: Detailed plan implementing FFTA-inspired elevation mechanics: asymmetric BFS climb/descent filtering, height-based damage bonus, fall damage on knockback, isometric renderer elevation, and Tiled map data with varied heights.

nodes.name: 047-line-of-sight-3d-collisions.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Phase-3 plan implementing 3D line-of-sight raycast with unified `heightBlocks`, MoveFlags aligned to Showdown, LoS integration across all attack patterns, 5 dedicated sandbox maps, and AoE preview updates in the renderer.

nodes.name: 048-pokedex-reference-knowledge-base.md
nodes.summary: Plan to build a local Pokedex reference knowledge base (JSON files for pokemon, moves, abilities, items, type-chart, and 19 inverse indexes) fetched from PokeAPI and Showdown, serving as the authoritative data source.

nodes.name: 049-migrate-game-data-to-reference.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2 of plan 048: migrates game data loading away from hardcoded TypeScript definitions to JSON loaders reading from the reference knowledge base, introducing `load-pokemon.ts` and `load-moves.ts` loaders.

nodes.name: 050-custom-tileset.md
nodes.summary: Plan to replace the JAO tileset with a custom Pokemon Mystery Dungeon-inspired isometric tileset, detailing tile format, terrain coverage, PixelLab generation approach, and the final 2026-04-13 implementation notes.

nodes.name: 051-terrain-types-modifiers.md
nodes.summary: Plan implementing terrain-type modifiers in the core engine: movement penalties via BFS, type/terrain damage bonuses, end-of-turn hazard handlers (magma burn, ice slide after knockback), and sandbox maps for each terrain.

nodes.name: 052-orientation-tactique.md
nodes.summary: Plan to implement FFTA-style directional combat bonuses and penalties (front, flank, back) in the core engine, adding orientation tracking to PokemonInstance and damage modifier computation.

nodes.name: 053-undo-movement.md
nodes.summary: Plan to implement movement undo in the core engine, allowing a Pokemon to cancel its move and return to its pre-move position as long as no action has been taken after moving.

nodes.name: 054-ct-system.md
nodes.summary: Plan introducing a Charge Time (CT) turn system as an alternative to round-robin, with a `TurnSystem` interface, `ChargeTimeTurnSystem` implementation driven by Speed and move cost tiers, BattleEngine integration, and maintenance flow for new moves.

nodes.name: 055-bug-gatling.md
nodes.summary: Bug-fix sprint plan targeting 5 game bugs across core and renderer, plus a STATUS.md cleanup to retire stale context.

nodes.name: 056-champions-data-update-process.md
nodes.summary: Plan establishing the Champions data update pipeline: sourcing from Showdown's `champions` mod and projectpokemon/champout, building a Champions override layer, `fetch-champions.ts` script, diff tooling, and npm scripts for reproducible updates.

nodes.name: 057-champions-status-runtime.md
nodes.summary: Plan wiring Champions-specific status rules (paralysis 12.5%, freeze 25%/3t, sleep sample([2,3,3])) into the core runtime via a `StatusRules` type, data loader, and updated `createStatusTickHandler`, replacing hardcoded constants.

nodes.name: 058-ct-timeline-preview.md
nodes.summary: Plan adding a CT timeline preview at attack confirmation: core `predictCtTimeline` method, renderer `TurnTimeline` preview overlay, and i18n separators for pre/post-attack turns.

nodes.name: 059-ct-timeline-preview.md
nodes.summary: Compact follow-up plan (amendment to plan 058) implementing a scrollable predictive CT timeline sequence with updated core types, TurnTimeline renderer constants, and i18n strings.

nodes.name: 060-occlusion-silhouette-cursor-ffta.md
nodes.summary: Two-section plan: Section A (delivered) adds an FFTA-style tile cursor to the renderer; Section B (stashed) adds occlusion silhouettes for Pokemon hidden behind terrain tiles, with worktree recovery instructions.

nodes.name: 062-3d-renderer-spike.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for spiking a 3D-geometry + billboard-sprite renderer using Three.js, evaluating four critical criteria: pixel-art fidelity, directional sprites under camera rotation, Tiled extrusion, and natural occlusion.

nodes.name: 063-babylon-spike.md
nodes.summary: Comparative spike plan for evaluating Babylon.js as an alternative to Three.js for the 2D-HD renderer, documenting the final verdict (Babylon retained), real measurements, and discovered gotchas from the 2026-04-18 spike.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains post-spike verdict and measured results, serving as both a plan and a decision record.

nodes.name: 064-decorations-obstacles.md
nodes.summary: Plan for adding Tiled-driven decoration and obstacle objects (tall grass, rocks, trees, arena markings) with 11 implementation steps covering parser changes, PixelLab asset generation, Ghost traversal rules, and Babylon renderer integration.

nodes.name: 065-occlusion-fade-depth-fix.md
nodes.summary: Plan for a unified depth fix, Alt-click picking disambiguation modifier, and a per-sprite dynamic fade occlusion system (OcclusionFader module), split into three sequential implementation parts with visual regression test checkpoints.

nodes.name: 066-maps-multi-format-roster.md
nodes.summary: Plan for reorganizing the maps asset directory, extending validateTiledMap with multi-format spawn layer support, and generating a first batch of AI-assisted themed arena maps with per-format objectgroup conventions.

nodes.name: 067-map-select-ui.md
nodes.summary: Plan for implementing a map selection screen with a preview scene, maps registry, and navigation wiring between TeamSelect and the battle scene, with i18n integration.

nodes.name: 068-ai-fix-terrain-ct-pathfinding.md

nodes.name: tiled-utils.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for findProperty and decodeTiledGid helpers, verifying property lookup and GID bitmask decoding logic.

nodes.name: tiled-utils.ts
nodes.summary: Low-level Tiled utility functions: findProperty for property bag lookups, and decodeTiledGid for extracting flip flags from raw GID integers.

nodes.name: findProperty
nodes.summary: Looks up a named property value from a Tiled property array, returning undefined when absent.

nodes.name: decodeTiledGid
nodes.summary: Decodes a raw Tiled GID integer by stripping the top three flip-flag bits, returning the local tile ID and flip booleans.

nodes.name: tileset-resolver.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for resolveTileProperties, verifying terrain assignment, height, slope, and flip-transform combinations from GIDs.

nodes.name: tileset-resolver.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves a raw Tiled GID to typed terrain properties (TerrainType, height, slope) by looking up tile metadata in the tileset and applying flip transforms.

nodes.name: resolveTileProperties
nodes.summary: Converts a raw GID and its tileset into a ResolvedTileProperties object, extracting terrain type, numeric height, and slope direction with flip correction.

nodes.name: validate-tiled-map.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for validateTiledMap covering spawn count validation, spawn connectivity checks, team format requirements, and spawn symmetry warnings.

nodes.name: validate-tiled-map.ts
nodes.summary: Validates a parsed MapDefinition for game correctness: checks required team formats, spawn terrain passability, BFS spawn connectivity, and spawn symmetry.

nodes.name: validateTiledMap
nodes.summary: Main validation entry point: checks each declared format's spawns for passability, BFS connectivity between teams, and emits warnings for asymmetric spawn counts.

nodes.name: checkSpawnConnectivity
nodes.summary: BFS-based connectivity check that verifies all team A spawns can reach at least one team B spawn through passable terrain.

nodes.name: type-chart.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the fully-loaded Pokemon type effectiveness chart as a typed Record keyed by PokemonType, loaded from the reference JSON via loadTypeChartFromReference.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the data package, extending the root tsconfig and including all source files while excluding node_modules.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite entry HTML for the renderer package: loads the custom PokemonEmeraldPro webfont, sets up a full-viewport game container, and bootstraps the Phaser application via src/main.ts.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses font-display:swap for the pixel-art game font and overflow:hidden to prevent scroll bars during fullscreen gameplay.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the renderer workspace, declaring dev/build/preview scripts via Vite and listing runtime dependencies (Phaser 4 and the core package).

nodes.name: debug-stack-4full.tmj

nodes.name: sandbox-fall-1.tmj

nodes.name: sandbox-fall-2.tmj

nodes.name: sandbox-fall-3.tmj

nodes.name: main

nodes.summary: CLI script that renders a full terrain column by invoking make-iso-tile.py for each tile shape variant (full, half, ramp, stairs) and assembling them vertically into a column image.
nodes.languageNotes: Orchestrates make-iso-tile.py as a subprocess; part of the 4-step terrain tileset pipeline.

nodes.name: render_shape
nodes.summary: Invokes make-iso-tile.py as a subprocess to render a single tile shape variant given top and side texture paths, writing the output to a given path.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for the terrain build script; parses CLI arguments, invokes render_shape for each tile shape, and composites the results into a single terrain column image.

nodes.name: download-status-icons.ts
nodes.summary: One-shot script that downloads status condition icon images (e.g. sleep, paralysis, burn) from a remote URL and writes them to the renderer public assets directory.

nodes.name: download-type-icons.ts
nodes.summary: One-shot script that downloads Pokemon type badge icons from a remote source and writes them to the renderer public assets directory in both colored and monochrome variants.

nodes.name: extract-pmd-tile.py
nodes.summary: Python script that lifts individual isometric tiles out of a PMD Spriters Resource sprite sheet by auto-detecting the tile grid origin and optionally generating a labeled scan overview image.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pillow for pixel-level grid detection; auto-detects tile boundary by scanning for non-empty rows/columns.

nodes.name: detect_y0
nodes.summary: Detects the first non-transparent row in a sprite sheet to identify the top boundary of the tile grid.

nodes.name: extract_tile
nodes.summary: Crops a single tile from the sprite sheet at the given grid column/row coordinates using detected or overridden grid origin values.

nodes.name: generate_scan
nodes.summary: Generates a labeled overview image of all tiles in the sheet, showing each non-empty tile at 2× scale with column/row index labels for easy identification.

nodes.name: extract-sprites.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive asset pipeline script that downloads Pokemon sprites from PMDCollab/SpriteCollab, parses AnimData.xml, packs animation frames into texture atlases, extracts portraits, and writes Phaser-compatible atlas JSON and offset metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sharp for pixel-level frame extraction and atlas compositing; fast-xml-parser for AnimData.xml; reads sprite-config.json for which Pokemon and animations to download.

nodes.name: extractPokemon
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full per-Pokemon extraction pipeline: fetches AnimData.xml, filters requested animations, extracts frames for each direction, builds and saves the atlas PNG/JSON, downloads portraits, and writes offset metadata.

nodes.name: parseAnimData
nodes.summary: Parses AnimData.xml content into a map of animation definitions, resolving CopyOf references to deduplicate animations that share frame data.

nodes.name: packFrames
nodes.summary: Bin-packs extracted sprite frames into a minimal atlas layout by sorting by size and placing frames row-by-row, returning the packed positions and total atlas dimensions.

nodes.name: parseOffsetsFromSheet
nodes.summary: Extracts pixel-level foot, head, and body offset positions from the shadow/offset sprite sheet for a given direction, used to compute runtime sprite anchoring data.

nodes.name: generate-golden-replay.ts
nodes.summary: Developer script that runs a deterministic battle simulation using a fixed seed and aggressive AI, then writes the resulting BattleReplay to a JSON golden fixture file used for replay regression testing.

nodes.name: buildGoldenEngine
nodes.summary: Constructs a fully configured BattleEngine with real game data, a seeded PRNG, two teams of hardcoded Pokemon, and a pre-built arena map for use in golden replay generation.

nodes.name: runGoldenBattle
nodes.summary: Runs the golden battle loop: each turn picks aggressive actions for all active Pokemon, submits them to the engine, and terminates when one team is eliminated or the round cap is reached, then exports the replay.

nodes.name: make-iso-tile.py
nodes.summary: Python script that renders isometric tile variants (full block, half block, south ramp, south stairs) from flat top and side textures using affine projection, with silhouette gap-filling for pixel-perfect edges.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements affine transformation from 3-corner correspondences to achieve the isometric projection; uses Pillow alpha compositing with per-face darkening for depth cues.

nodes.name: render_full
nodes.summary: Renders a full-height isometric block by projecting the top face and both side faces (left darker, right darkest) onto a canvas using affine transforms.

nodes.name: render_half
nodes.summary: Renders a half-height isometric block, used for terrain elevation transitions, projecting scaled-down faces onto the canvas.

nodes.name: render_ramp_s
nodes.summary: Renders a south-facing ramp tile where the top face is sheared to slope from ground level on the south edge to full height on the north edge.

nodes.name: render_stairs_s
nodes.summary: Renders a south-facing staircase tile by compositing multiple step faces in painter's order, each computed via isometric coordinate projection.

nodes.name: affine_from_3_corners
nodes.summary: Computes a 6-parameter affine transformation matrix from three source and destination corner correspondences for use with Pillow's transform method.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md is the front door: Pokemon Tactics is a tactical RPG blending Gen-1 Pokemon with Final Fantasy Tactics mechanics, built on TypeScript + Phaser 4 as a monorepo. Reading it first gives you the three-package structure (core, data, renderer), the design philosophy of a UI-free engine, and the progression status of the roster. This sets the mental model for everything that follows.

tour.title: Architecture Contract
tour.description: docs/architecture.md explains the core decoupling principle that governs the whole codebase: the battle engine emits events, the renderer observes them — they never call each other directly. It documents the monorepo layout, the attack-pattern taxonomy, and the one-way dependency rule (renderer imports core, core imports nothing). Understanding this contract before reading any code prevents misreading the design intent.

tour.title: Game Design Rules
tour.description: docs/game-design.md is the source of truth for all mechanics: the Charge Time (CT) turn order, the damage formula, terrain height bonuses, and status conditions. Every number in the engine traces back here. Reading the design document before the implementation stops you from wondering why the engine does something unusual — it is usually a deliberate tactical design choice.

tour.title: Core Package Public API
tour.description: packages/core/src/index.ts is the barrel that defines what the core package exports to the outside world. It re-exports BattleEngine, all AI modules, grid utilities, enums, and types. With fan-out of 31, it is the single seam between the engine and every consumer. Scanning this file tells you the full surface area of the core at a glance without reading every module.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript barrel files use 'export * from' to aggregate many modules into one public entry point, creating a stable API surface. Consumers import from the package name rather than deep file paths, which isolates them from internal refactors.

tour.title: Domain Types: Pokemon and Moves
tour.description: PokemonInstance and MoveDefinition are the two most-referenced types in the engine (fan-in 44 and 36 respectively). PokemonInstance holds the full runtime state of a Pokemon on the battlefield — HP, stat stages, active statuses, position, CT accumulator. MoveDefinition is the data contract for a move: power, accuracy, PP, targeting pattern, and the effect list the engine will execute. Together these two types are the vocabulary of every mechanic.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript discriminated unions (a field like 'kind' with a literal type) allow the compiler to narrow a union to its exact member inside an if/switch block. MoveDefinition uses this pattern for effect kinds, giving exhaustive type checking when adding new effects.

tour.title: BattleEngine: Central Orchestrator
tour.description: BattleEngine.ts (1936 lines, fan-out 66, fan-in 28) is the heart of the engine. It manages turn flow, validates and executes actions, advances the CT system, detects KOs, and emits BattleEvents after every state change. It deliberately owns no rendering logic — all output is structured events. This is the most complex file in the project and the one that ties all the subsystems together.
tour.languageLesson: At this scale, a class that touches many subsystems is kept coherent by expressing all output as an append-only event log (BattleEvent[]). Callers replay the log to derive UI state, replay battles deterministically, or feed an AI scorer — without BattleEngine knowing which consumer is listening.

tour.title: Effect Handler Registry: No Switch/Case
tour.description: effect-handler-registry.ts and effect-processor.ts implement the core design pattern for move execution. Instead of a giant switch statement, each EffectKind (damage, stat-change, status, heal, knockback, etc.) is registered as a named handler function. effect-processor.ts looks up the handler by kind and dispatches it with type-effectiveness and immunity filtering already applied. Adding a new move effect means writing one handler and one registration — zero changes to existing code.
tour.languageLesson: The registry pattern (Map<EffectKind, HandlerFn>) is a form of the command pattern. It makes the set of valid operations open for extension and closed for modification — exactly the Open/Closed Principle. TypeScript's const enum ensures only valid keys can be registered.

tour.title: Effect Handlers: Damage and Status
tour.description: handle-damage.ts and the status-tick-handler are the two most-exercised handlers. handle-damage.ts resolves multi-hit counts, applies type effectiveness, criticals, and recoil, then emits a Damaged event per hit. status-tick-handler.ts fires each end-of-turn status effect (burn, poison, sleep countdown, paralysis rolls) and emits the corresponding events. These files are the best illustration of how a single EffectKind maps to a concrete mechanic.

tour.title: CT Turn System
tour.description: ChargeTimeTurnSystem.ts and TurnManager.ts implement the FFTA-style turn order: every Pokemon accumulates Charge Time each tick and acts when it crosses a threshold. ChargeTimeTurnSystem tracks the timeline and computes whose turn is next, while TurnManager wraps it to expose a simple advance/peek API to BattleEngine. This is why faster Pokemon act more frequently — the CT delta between them compounds across many turns.

tour.title: BattleEvent: The Observable State Bus
tour.description: BattleEventType (fan-in 129) and BattleEvent together form the event vocabulary. BattleEventType is a const enum listing every observable action: Moved, Damaged, StatusApplied, StatChanged, KO, etc. BattleEvent is the discriminated union where each variant carries the typed payload for that event. The renderer subscribes to this stream to animate; the AI replays it to evaluate moves; the golden-replay test records and replays it for regression testing.

tour.title: Isometric Grid and Targeting
tour.description: Grid.ts manages the 2D tile map with terrain type, elevation, and occupant data for every cell. targeting.ts implements all move targeting patterns — single target, cone, line, area blast, slash, cross — with height-aware line-of-sight calculations. The grid is the spatial substrate that makes Pokemon Tactics a tactical game: positioning, elevation bonuses, and terrain type all feed directly into the damage formula from Step 3.

tour.title: Data Package: Loading and Overrides
tour.description: packages/data/src/load-data.ts reads the canonical Pokemon Champions reference JSON files and merges tactical.ts overrides onto top of them. The overrides layer (979 lines) patches base stats, movesets, and abilities for Gen-1 Pokemon to suit tactical gameplay — for example giving Pokemon four-move limits or adjusting stat values. This two-layer approach lets the reference data remain the authoritative Pokemon source while the game customizes it without forking.

tour.title: Renderer Entry Point and GameController
tour.description: packages/renderer/src/main.ts bootstraps the Phaser 4 game instance and assembles all scenes. GameController.ts (2064 lines, fan-out 23) is the central Phaser scene that handles all user interaction: tile clicks, hover previews, action menus, placement phase, and rendering Pokemon sprites. It subscribes to BattleEngine events and translates them into animations and UI updates — this is the boundary where the decoupled architecture from Step 2 becomes concrete.

tour.title: AI System: Scoring and Strategy
tour.description: action-scorer.ts evaluates every legal action available to an AI player by simulating the outcome — expected damage, positional gain, status value — and producing a numeric score. scored-ai.ts ranks all actions by score, then picks stochastically from the top candidates so the AI is strong but not perfectly predictable. The AI system is decoupled from rendering and can run headless at full speed, which is how the balancer agent tests game balance.

tour.title: Testing Infrastructure and CI
tour.description: packages/core/src/testing/index.ts provides the shared test factory toolkit: buildMoveTestEngine for per-move scenario tests, MockBattle for minimal BattleState construction, and mock-map helpers. Every move in the game has a dedicated .test.ts file using these factories. The CI pipeline (.github/workflows/ci.yml) gates every push with lint, typecheck, unit tests, and integration tests — ensuring the pure engine invariants are never broken by a renderer or data change.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions CI uses pnpm caching keyed on the lockfile hash to avoid reinstalling all workspace packages on every run. The job matrix could later test across Node versions. Separating unit and integration test steps makes it easier to pinpoint which layer a regression comes from.
